claim: IPL1 copies IPL2 from console PIF ROM into RSP SP IMEM.
evidence: Pinned N64-IPL IPL1 source copies the span delimited by ipl2_rom and ipl2_rom_end to SP IMEM start.
corroboration: N64 architecture analysis and Mupen64Plus boot HLE
contradiction: Mupen HLE copies only the x105-required prefix and is not full hardware causality.

claim: The CPU executes IPL2 from SP IMEM.
evidence: Pinned IPL1 source jumps to SP IMEM start after the copy; IPL2 is anchored there.
corroboration: CEN64 requires and executes user-supplied PIF data.
contradiction: n64docs collapses IPL1/IPL2 into simulated side effects.

claim: IPL2 copies IPL3 from cartridge into SP DMEM and transfers CPU execution to the bootcode entry.
evidence: Pinned IPL2 source copies cartridge 0x040..0x1000 to equal SP DMEM offsets, verifies it, and jumps to SP DMEM 0x040.
corroboration: Current fn64 staging and private trace reach 0xA4000040.
contradiction: n64docs describes a simplified whole-0x1000 copy.
notes: Current fn64 range matches the stronger source.

claim: When IPL3 begins, some or all IPL2 content remains resident in SP IMEM.
evidence: No source write clears SP IMEM between IPL1 copy and IPL3 jump; x105 IPL3 reads and writes the retained prefix.
corroboration: Mupen HLE materializes the required retained prefix.
contradiction: Complete numeric IPL2 length and unobserved PIF revisions remain unknown.
notes: Confirmed for at least the complete x105-consumed prefix.

claim: The Lw at 0xA4000044 intentionally reads retained IPL2 content at 0xA4001000.
evidence: The authentic instruction shape matches the pinned x105 entry prelude, whose first data read targets retained SP IMEM offset zero.
corroboration: Current fn64 runtime routes the exact effective address to SP IMEM offset zero.
contradiction: Exact word value is proprietary firmware content and remains unavailable.
notes: Not reset zero, cartridge data, or game DMA.

claim: The selected bootcode is a variant whose initial instructions consume and modify retained SP IMEM words.
evidence: External private bootcode-family classification matches the x105 family; pinned x105 source reads and rewrites eight retained words and writes three more.
source_anchor: src/ipl3.s:72-105; n64checksum README Different checksum types
corroboration: Mupen64Plus bootrom_hle.c:133-141 seeds exactly the eight-word required prefix.
contradiction: PIF revision matrix is unknown; emulator HLE is corroboration only.
notes: Private digest remains external evidence only.

claim: Representing only offset zero would be incomplete if the bootstrap sequence consumes a larger contiguous region.
evidence: The x105 loop consumes SP IMEM [0x000,0x020), rewrites it, and writes through 0x02b.
corroboration: Mupen64Plus bootrom_hle.c:133-141 covers eight input words.
contradiction: None material; exact values remain forbidden.
notes: One-word staging would clear only the first failure and falsify the following state.
